response:
# Data Table Testing Accessibility Design Plan > **For Claude:** Use a11y-planner protocol. Review with a11y-critic after testing plan is implemented. > **Compliance target:** WCAG 2.2 AA > **Users who need accessibility:** Screen reader users, keyboard-only users, low vision users, QA engineers validating accessibility behavior > **Assistive technologies:** NVDA + Chrome primary, JAWS + Chrome/Edge secondary, VoiceOver + Safari secondary, keyboard-only users **Feature:** Accessibility testing plan for a sortable, paginated financial transactions data table with per-row action menus. **Risk Level:** High **Component/Page Type:** Data Table, Sortable Headers, Pagination, Menu Button Dropdowns --- ## Scope & Context This plan covers testing for a React `DataTable` component used in a financial reporting dashboard. The table displays 50 transaction records per page across seven columns: Date, Description, Category, Amount, Status, Account, and Actions. The component is high risk because it combines several accessibility-sensitive patterns: - Semantic data table navigation - Sortable column headers with `aria-sort` - Dynamic sort announcements through a polite live region - Pagination with focus persistence - Per-row action menus using the APG Menu Button pattern - Horizontal scrolling at small viewports - Screen reader table navigation expectations The goal is not merely to check whether axe passes. Axe can catch invalid table relationships and some ARIA issues, but it cannot fully verify screen reader table mode behavior, live region announcements, focus restoration, or menu keyboard interaction. Those must be tested manually and with Playwright keyboard assertions. What this plan is claiming: the table can meet WCAG 2.2 AA if the tests below pass. What this plan is not claiming: passing these tests proves every financial-dashboard workflow is accessible. This plan covers the `DataTable` component surface only: table semantics, sort, pagination, responsive behavior, and row actions. --- ## Semantic Structure Plan The table should use native table semantics, not ARIA grid semantics. Because users are reading tabular financial records rather than editing cells in a spreadsheet-like interface, a native `` is the correct pattern. Required semantic structure: ```html

Transaction Records

Sort and pagination status messages
Transaction Records - 742 results
Date Description Category Amount Status Account Actions
... ... ... ... ... ... Row action menu button and menu
Previous and Next controls
``` Semantic requirements: | Element | Requirement | WCAG Citation | |---|---|---| | Table | Use native ``, ``, `` | WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships | | Caption | Include visible or programmatic caption: “Transaction Records - 742 results” | WCAG 1.3.1, 2.4.6 Headings and Labels | | Column headers | Use ``; do not omit because the column contains buttons | WCAG 1.3.1 | | Pagination | Wrap controls in `
` | WCAG 1.3.1, 2.4.6 | | Live region | Use one shared `role="status"` or `aria-live="polite"` region for sort/page messages | WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages | Do not require `headers=""` on every `td` for this table. The table has a flat, single-level column-header structure, so `scope="col"` is sufficient and less brittle. `headers` should be reserved for complex multi-level headers or irregular tables. --- ## Interaction Pattern Design ### Interactive Elements Table | Widget | APG Pattern | Keyboard Model | ARIA Attributes | WCAG Citation | |---|---|---|---|---| | Sortable Date header | APG Button pattern inside table header: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/button/ | Tab to button, Enter/Space cycles sort | `aria-sort` on active sortable `
` for all seven headers | WCAG 1.3.1 | | Row cells | Use `` for transaction values | WCAG 1.3.1 | | Actions header | Use `Actions`; button has accessible name such as “Sort by Date” | 2.1.1 Keyboard, 2.4.6 Headings and Labels, 4.1.2 Name Role Value | | Sortable Amount header | APG Button pattern | Tab, Enter, Space | `aria-sort` on Amount `` | 2.1.1, 4.1.2 | | Sortable Status header | APG Button pattern | Tab, Enter, Space | `aria-sort` on Status `` | 2.1.1, 4.1.2 | | Pagination Previous | Native Button | Tab, Enter, Space | Native `disabled` when unavailable; optional `aria-disabled` only for custom controls | 2.1.1, 4.1.2 | | Pagination Next | Native Button | Tab, Enter, Space | Native `disabled` when unavailable | 2.1.1, 4.1.2 | | Page indicator | Status text | Not interactive | Optional `aria-live="polite"` only if page changes are not otherwise announced | 4.1.3 | | Row Actions button | APG Menu Button: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/menu-button/ | Tab to button, Enter/Space opens menu, Down Arrow may open and focus first item | `aria-haspopup="menu"`, `aria-expanded`, `aria-controls` | 2.1.1, 2.1.2, 4.1.2 | | Row action menu | APG Menu: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/menubar/ | Up/Down moves between menu items, Enter/Space activates, Escape closes and restores focus | `role="menu"`, `role="menuitem"` | 2.1.1, 2.4.3, 4.1.2 | ### Sort Behavior Each sortable column cycles: 1. No sort 2. Ascending 3. Descending 4. No sort Acceptance-level behavior: - Clicking Date once sets Date `aria-sort="ascending"`. - Clicking Date again sets Date `aria-sort="descending"`. - Clicking Date again removes sorting or sets Date `aria-sort="none"`, depending on implementation convention. - Only the currently sorted column may expose `aria-sort="ascending"` or `aria-sort="descending"`. - Non-sorted sortable columns should either omit `aria-sort` or use `aria-sort="none"` consistently. - The live region announces each transition, for example: - “Sorted by Date, ascending” - “Sorted by Date, descending” - “Date sorting cleared” ### Row Action Menu Behavior Each row’s Actions button must follow the APG Menu Button pattern. Expected behavior: - On focus, screen reader announces a row-specific name, role, and state, such as “Actions for transaction Coffee Shop, button, collapsed, menu.” - Enter or Space opens the menu. - Down Arrow may open the menu and move focus to the first item. - Menu items are Edit, Delete, View Details. - Up/Down Arrow moves between menu items. - Home moves to first item; End moves to last item if implemented. - Escape closes the menu and restores focus to the row’s Actions button. - Tab should either close the menu and move to the next tabbable element, or keep focus management consistent with the chosen menu implementation. The preferred APG behavior is that menu navigation is arrow-key based while open. - Opening one row menu closes any other open row menu. --- ## Focus Management Plan ### Default Tab Order Expected keyboard order: 1. Skip link or page-level navigation, if present 2. Dashboard heading or relevant filters before table, if present 3. Sortable Date header button 4. Sortable Amount header button 5. Sortable Status header button 6. Row 1 Actions button 7. Row 2 Actions button 8. Remaining visible row Actions buttons 9. Previous page button 10. Next page button 11. Next dashboard control after the table Native table data cells should not be individually tabbable. Screen reader users navigate cells through table navigation commands. Keyboard-only users tab to actionable controls only. ### Sort Focus After activating a sortable header: - Focus remains on the activated sort button. - The `aria-sort` state updates on the corresponding ``. - The live region announces the new sort. - Focus must not jump to the top of the page or first table row. WCAG grounding: 2.4.3 Focus Order, 3.2.2 On Input, 4.1.3 Status Messages. ### Pagination Focus After activating Previous or Next: - Focus stays on the activated pagination button when it remains enabled. - If the activated button becomes disabled after the page change, focus should move to the nearest available pagination control or to the page indicator with `tabindex="-1"` only if a clear announcement is needed. - Page change is announced through the shared polite status region, for example: “Page 2 of 15 loaded, showing transactions 51 to 100.” - Do not move focus into the first table cell automatically. That disrupts keyboard users and screen reader users who intentionally activated pagination. WCAG grounding: 2.4.3 Focus Order, 4.1.3 Status Messages. ### Row Menu Focus When a row Actions menu opens: - Focus moves to the first menu item, preferably Edit. - Focus remains inside the open menu while using arrow keys. - Escape closes the menu and restores focus to the triggering Actions button. - Selecting View Details, Edit, or Delete follows the destination behavior for that action. - If Delete removes a row from the current page, focus should move to the next row’s Actions button. If the deleted row was the last row on the page, focus moves to the previous row’s Actions button. If the page becomes empty, focus moves to the pagination region or empty-state message. WCAG grounding: 2.1.1 Keyboard, 2.1.2 No Keyboard Trap, 2.4.3 Focus Order. ### Horizontal Scroll Focus The horizontal scroll container may be focusable only if keyboard users need to scroll it directly. If focusable: - It must have an accessible name such as “Transaction records table scroll area.” - Arrow keys or Shift + mouse wheel behavior should not trap focus. - Tab from the scroll container must proceed to the next interactive control. - Full page horizontal scrolling must not occur at 200% zoom; only the table region may scroll horizontally if necessary. WCAG grounding: 1.4.10 Reflow, 2.1.1 Keyboard, 2.1.2 No Keyboard Trap. --- ## State Communication Design ### State Communication Table | State | Visual Indicator | Programmatic Indicator | ARIA Attribute | WCAG Citation | |---|---|---|---|---| | Sort none | Neutral sort icon or no active indicator | Header is not sorted | Omit `aria-sort` or set `aria-sort="none"` | 4.1.2 | | Sort ascending | Up arrow icon plus active styling | Sorted column and direction exposed | `aria-sort="ascending"` on `` | 1.3.1, 4.1.2 | | Sort descending | Down arrow icon plus active styling | Sorted column and direction exposed | `aria-sort="descending"` on `` | 1.3.1, 4.1.2 | | Sort changed | Visible order changes | Polite live announcement | `role="status"` or `aria-live="polite"` | 4.1.3 | | Page loading | Loading spinner/text | Busy state on table region if async | `aria-busy="true"` during load | 4.1.3 | | Page loaded | Page indicator updates | Polite live announcement | `role="status"` | 4.1.3 | | Previous unavailable | Button appears disabled | Native disabled state | `disabled` | 4.1.2 | | Next unavailable | Button appears disabled | Native disabled state | `disabled` | 4.1.2 | | Row menu closed | Menu hidden, chevron neutral | Button state collapsed | `aria-expanded="false"` | 4.1.2 | | Row menu open | Menu visible | Button state expanded; menu exposed | `aria-expanded="true"`, `role="menu"` | 4.1.2 | | Menu item focused | Visible focus ring | Programmatic focus on item | DOM focus | 2.4.7, 2.4.13 | | Delete unavailable | Disabled menu item styling | Disabled state | `aria-disabled="true"` if menu item remains focusable | 4.1.2 | | Horizontal overflow | Scrollbar or gradient edge | Named scroll region if focusable | `aria-label` on scroll container if `tabindex="0"` | 2.4.6, 4.1.2 | Use one live region for sort and pagination events. Do not create a live region per row, per cell, or per menu. Repeated live regions in 50-row tables create noisy and unpredictable screen reader output. Sort icons such as ▲, ▼, +, or - should be `aria-hidden="true"` when the state is already communicated through `aria-sort`. Otherwise screen readers may announce redundant symbols. --- ## Visual Accessibility Plan Color and contrast requirements: - Body text and cell text must meet 4.5:1 contrast minimum. - Large text must meet 3:1 contrast minimum. - Table borders, input outlines, buttons, menu boundaries, and focus indicators must meet 3:1 non-text contrast. - Focus indicators should use a two-color ring when possible, such as a dark outline plus light offset shadow. - Use `:focus-visible` so keyboard focus is visible without creating mouse-click noise. - Sort state cannot be conveyed by color alone. Use text, icon shape, `aria-sort`, and live-region announcement. - Status values such as Pending, Failed, Cleared, or Reconciled cannot rely on color alone. Use visible text labels and, if icons are used, icons must not be the only indicator. - Action buttons must have a minimum target size of 24x24 CSS pixels under WCAG 2.5.8; 44x44 is recommended for row menus. - At 200% zoom, the table must remain usable. The page itself should not horizontally scroll; the table container may scroll horizontally. - At 400% zoom / 320px CSS equivalent, all functions must remain available, including sort, pagination, and row actions. WCAG citations: 1.4.1 Use of Color, 1.4.3 Contrast Minimum, 1.4.10 Reflow, 1.4.11 Non-text Contrast, 2.4.7 Focus Visible, 2.4.11 Focus Not Obscured, 2.4.13 Focus Appearance, 2.5.8 Target Size. --- ## Content Accessibility Plan ### Table Caption The caption must be available to screen readers and should preferably be visible: “Transaction Records - 742 results” If visual design hides it, use a visually hidden caption, not `aria-label` alone. Native captions improve table discovery in screen reader table lists. WCAG: 1.3.1, 2.4.6. ### Sort Button Names Sortable headers need clear accessible names. Acceptable names: - “Sort by Date” - “Sort by Amount” - “Sort by Status” When sorted, do not cram the full state into the button name if `aria-sort` and the live region already communicate state. Avoid names like “Date ascending sorted ascending” due to duplicate announcements. ### Row Action Button Names Each row action button must be unique enough for screen reader users. Acceptable: - “Actions for transaction Coffee Shop on March 14” - “Actions for transaction Payroll Deposit on March 15” Not acceptable: - “Actions” - “More” - “Menu” - Title-only labels Use visible text if possible. If the button is icon-only, use `aria-label`, not `title`. ### Menu Item Names Menu items should be plain, actionable text: - Edit - Delete - View Details If repeated actions need row context, either the menu button provides context or the menu item accessible name includes it. Avoid excessive repetition if the screen reader context is already clear. ### Language and Reading Order - Page root must have `lang="en"`. - DOM order must match visual order. - Do not visually reorder columns through CSS while leaving DOM order unchanged. - Currency amounts should be readable. For example, `$1,250.00` is usually acceptable, but negative amounts should include a visible minus sign or parentheses with clear financial convention. WCAG: 1.3.2 Meaningful Sequence, 3.1.1 Language of Page, 2.4.6 Headings and Labels. --- ## Testing Strategy Testing should run in this order because failures earlier in the stack can invalidate later screen reader findings. ### 1. Automated Runtime Scan Tooling: - Playwright - axe-core injected into rendered page - Run in Chromium first, then optionally WebKit for VoiceOver-adjacent rendering issues Required axe rules to include or explicitly verify: - `td-headers-attr` - `th-has-data-cells` - `scope-attr-valid` - `table-duplicate-name` - `aria-valid-attr` - `aria-valid-attr-value` - `aria-allowed-attr` - `aria-required-children` - `aria-required-parent` - `button-name` - `color-contrast` - `landmark-one-main` - `region` Expected result: - No serious or critical axe violations. - No table-specific rule failures. - No unnamed buttons in sortable headers, pagination, or row actions. ### 2. DOM Semantics Verification A QA engineer should verify rendered DOM, not just React component props. Check: - Table has exactly one useful caption. - All seven column headers are ``. - Date, Amount, and Status sortable headers expose valid sort state. - Non-sortable headers do not falsely expose active sort state. - The Actions header exists and is scoped. - Row action buttons have unique accessible names. - Menu roles appear only when the menu is open, unless the implementation keeps menus mounted and properly hidden with `hidden`, `inert`, or equivalent. Acceptance criteria: - Header associations work through native table semantics. - No data table uses `role="presentation"`. - No row/cell semantics are replaced with non-table elements. - No `headers` attributes are required for this flat table unless the implementation introduces multi-level headers. ### 3. Sort State Playwright Tests Script the state cycle for each sortable header. Date example: 1. Locate Date sortable button. 2. Press Enter or click. 3. Assert Date `` has `aria-sort="ascending"`. 4. Assert live region text contains “Sorted by Date, ascending.” 5. Activate again. 6. Assert Date `` has `aria-sort="descending"`. 7. Assert live region text contains “Sorted by Date, descending.” 8. Activate again. 9. Assert Date `` has `aria-sort="none"` or no `aria-sort`, depending on the documented implementation. 10. Assert live region text contains “Date sorting cleared” or equivalent. Repeat for Amount and Status. Cross-column test: - Sort Date ascending. - Sort Amount ascending. - Assert Amount is active. - Assert Date no longer has `aria-sort="ascending"` or `aria-sort="descending"`. Acceptance criteria: - Sort cycles none → ascending → descending → none. - Only one column has active sort direction at a time. - Live region updates for every transition. - Focus remains on activated sort button. ### 4. Keyboard Navigation Testing Test with keyboard only. Sortable headers: - Tab reaches each sortable header button. - Enter activates sort. - Space activates sort. - Focus indicator is visible. - Focus does not move unexpectedly after sort. Table body: - Tab does not enter static data cells. - Tab reaches each row action button in logical row order. - Shift+Tab reverses order predictably. Pagination: - Tab reaches Previous and Next. - Enter and Space activate each enabled button. - Focus remains on the activated button after page change unless it becomes disabled. - Disabled Previous on page 1 is not keyboard focusable if native `disabled` is used. - Disabled Next on final page is not keyboard focusable if native `disabled` is used. Horizontal scroll: - At narrow viewport, the table scroll container can be operated without trapping keyboard focus. - The full page does not require horizontal scrolling at 200% zoom. - Focused row action buttons are not clipped or hidden behind sticky columns, headers, or scroll shadows. ### 5. NVDA + Chrome Screen Reader Testing Primary manual combination: NVDA + Chrome. Distinguish these modes clearly for QA: - **Browse Mode:** NVDA’s normal page-reading mode. Used to navigate headings, tables, buttons, and text. - **Table navigation commands:** While in Browse Mode, use table commands to move cell by cell. NVDA steps: 1. Open the dashboard page in Chrome with NVDA running. 2. Use `T` to move to the table. 3. Confirm NVDA announces the table caption: “Transaction Records - 742 results.” 4. Use `Ctrl+Alt+Right Arrow` to move across cells. 5. Use `Ctrl+Alt+Left Arrow` to move backward. 6. Use `Ctrl+Alt+Down Arrow` to move down a column. 7. Use `Ctrl+Alt+Up Arrow` to move up a column. 8. Confirm NVDA announces the relevant column header with each cell. 9. Move to cells in Amount and Status columns and confirm headers are announced. 10. Move to the Actions column and confirm the cell context includes Actions. Expected result: - NVDA announces table structure, caption, row/column movement, and column headers. - Header context is preserved across rows. - Sort controls are discoverable as buttons when navigating to headers. - Static cells are readable without requiring Tab. Sort announcement test with NVDA: 1. Tab to Date sort button. 2. Press Enter. 3. Confirm NVDA announces the state change or the live region message: “Sorted by Date, ascending.” 4. Repeat for descending and cleared states. ### 6. JAWS and VoiceOver Spot Checks JAWS: - Confirm table caption and headers are announced. - Confirm sort controls expose button role and sort state. - Confirm row menu opens, arrow navigation works, and Escape restores focus. VoiceOver macOS: - Confirm table can be discovered and navigated. - Confirm sortable headers are announced as controls. - Confirm menu button expanded/collapsed state is announced. - Confirm pagination update is announced or discoverable without focus loss. These are secondary checks. Any critical mismatch from NVDA should still be investigated because the component must support all listed ATs. ### 7. Per-Row Menu Testing For first row, middle row, and last visible row: 1. Tab to row Actions button. 2. Confirm accessible name includes row context. 3. Press Enter. 4. Assert menu opens. 5. Assert button has `aria-expanded="true"`. 6. Assert focus moves to first menu item. 7. Press Down Arrow. 8. Assert focus moves to Delete. 9. Press Down Arrow. 10. Assert focus moves to View Details. 11. Press Up Arrow. 12. Assert focus moves back to Delete. 13. Press Escape. 14. Assert menu closes. 15. Assert `aria-expanded="false"`. 16. Assert focus returns to the same row Actions button. Acceptance criteria: - Menu follows APG Menu Button behavior. - Focus does not fall into hidden menus from other rows. - Opening a second row menu closes the first. - Screen reader announces menu item names clearly. - No menu item relies on icon-only names. ### 8. Pagination Testing Test pages 1, 2, and final page. Page 1: - Previous is disabled. - Next is enabled. - Page indicator says “Page 1 of 15.” - Activating Next updates table rows and page indicator. - Focus remains on Next. - Live region announces “Page 2 of 15 loaded” or equivalent. Middle page: - Previous and Next are enabled. - Activating Previous returns to prior page. - Activating Next advances to next page. - Focus remains stable. - Table caption result count remains accurate. Final page: - Next is disabled. - Previous is enabled. - If activating Next is impossible, it must not be focusable or must clearly expose disabled state if custom. - Page indicator says “Page 15 of 15.” Acceptance criteria: - Pagination can be completed with keyboard only. - Page changes are announced. - Focus is predictable. - Disabled state is programmatic, not visual only. ### 9. Responsive and Zoom Testing Viewports: - Desktop: 1440px wide - Tablet/narrow: 768px wide - Mobile-equivalent: 390px wide - 200% browser zoom - 400% zoom equivalent / 320px CSS layout check Tests: - Table container scrolls horizontally when needed. - Full page does not horizontally scroll at 200% zoom. - Column headers remain available and associated with cells. - Sort buttons remain reachable. - Row Actions buttons remain reachable. - Pagination remains visible and keyboard accessible. - Focus indicators are not clipped by overflow containers. - Sticky headers or sticky columns do not obscure focused elements. Acceptance criteria: - No functionality disappears at narrow widths. - Horizontal table scroll does not create a keyboard trap. - Header association remains intact. - Focused controls are at least partially visible per WCAG 2.4.11. --- ## Test Cases - Automated axe scan reports no serious or critical violations. - Axe table rules pass: `td-headers-attr`, `th-has-data-cells`, `scope-attr-valid`, `table-duplicate-name`. - DOM check confirms all column headers use ``. - DOM check confirms table caption is present and meaningful. - Date sort cycles none → ascending → descending → none. - Amount sort cycles none → ascending → descending → none. - Status sort cycles none → ascending → descending → none. - Live region announces each sort transition. - Only one column exposes active `aria-sort` at a time. - Keyboard Tab reaches sortable headers, row action buttons, and pagination controls in logical order. - Static cells are not inserted into the Tab order. - NVDA table navigation announces column headers with cells. - Pagination updates page indicator and live region. - Pagination keeps focus on activated button unless disabled. - Row menu opens with Enter and Space. - Row menu supports Up/Down Arrow navigation. - Escape closes row menu and restores focus to trigger button. - At 200% zoom, the page does not horizontally scroll. - At narrow viewport, table horizontal scroll remains keyboard usable. - Focus indicators are visible and not clipped. --- ## Implementation Tasks ### Task 1: Automated Accessibility Test Harness 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Files:** - Playwright accessibility test file for DataTable - Shared axe helper if project uses one - QA documentation page or checklist **Structure Stub:** ```text DataTable page renders axe-core injected scan runs against table default state scan repeats after sort, menu open, and paginated state ``` **ARIA Attributes to Verify:** - `aria-sort`: valid values only; WCAG 4.1.2 - `aria-live` or `role="status"`: present for sort/page messages; WCAG 4.1.3 - `aria-expanded`: row menu button state; WCAG 4.1.2 - `aria-haspopup="menu"`: row menu button; WCAG 4.1.2 - `aria-controls`: row menu relationship if used; WCAG 4.1.2 **Tests:** - Run axe on default table. - Run axe after Date sort. - Run axe after opening first row menu. - Run axe after pagination to page 2. - Assert table-specific axe rules pass. **WCAG Criteria:** - 1.3.1 Info and Relationships - 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value - 4.1.3 Status Messages --- ### Task 2: Table Semantics Verification 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Files:** - DataTable semantic tests - QA manual checklist **Structure Stub:** ```text section labelled by heading table with caption thead with seven th scope="col" tbody with td cells Actions column header present pagination nav labelled ``` **ARIA Attributes:** - Avoid unnecessary ARIA where native table semantics suffice. - `aria-label` only for pagination nav or scroll region if needed; WCAG 2.4.6, 4.1.2. **Tests:** - Verify caption text equals or includes “Transaction Records - 742 results.” - Verify seven column headers. - Verify each header has `scope="col"`. - Verify Actions header exists. - Verify no table has `role="presentation"`. - Verify no static cell is tabbable. **WCAG Criteria:** - 1.3.1 Info and Relationships - 1.3.2 Meaningful Sequence - 2.4.6 Headings and Labels --- ### Task 3: Sort State and Announcement Tests 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Files:** - DataTable sort Playwright tests - QA sort checklist **Structure Stub:** ```text sortable th contains button active th exposes aria-sort shared status region announces sort change focus remains on activated sort button ``` **ARIA Attributes:** - `aria-sort="ascending"`: active ascending column; WCAG 4.1.2 - `aria-sort="descending"`: active descending column; WCAG 4.1.2 - `aria-sort="none"` or omitted: unsorted state; WCAG 4.1.2 - `role="status"` or `aria-live="polite"`: sort announcements; WCAG 4.1.3 **Keyboard Interactions:** - Tab: focus sortable header button. - Enter: cycle sort. - Space: cycle sort. **Tests:** - Date sort full cycle. - Amount sort full cycle. - Status sort full cycle. - Cross-column active sort exclusivity. - Live region message after every transition. - Focus remains on sort control. **WCAG Criteria:** - 2.1.1 Keyboard - 2.4.3 Focus Order - 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value - 4.1.3 Status Messages --- ### Task 4: Pagination Behavior Tests 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Files:** - DataTable pagination Playwright tests - QA pagination checklist **Structure Stub:** ```text nav aria-label="Transaction records pagination" Previous button Page 2 of 15 indicator Next button shared status region announces page load ``` **ARIA Attributes:** - `aria-label` on pagination nav; WCAG 2.4.6 - `disabled` on unavailable native buttons; WCAG 4.1.2 - `role="status"` or `aria-live="polite"` for page changes; WCAG 4.1.3 **Keyboard Interactions:** - Tab: focus Previous/Next. - Enter/Space: activate enabled button. - Shift+Tab: reverse navigation. **Tests:** - Page 1 disabled Previous. - Middle page Previous/Next both enabled. - Final page disabled Next. - Focus remains on activated button after page change. - Live region announces new page. - Rows update without losing table semantics. **WCAG Criteria:** - 2.1.1 Keyboard - 2.4.3 Focus Order - 3.2.2 On Input - 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value - 4.1.3 Status Messages --- ### Task 5: Row Action Menu Tests 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Files:** - DataTable row menu Playwright tests - QA menu checklist **Structure Stub:** ```text button aria-haspopup="menu" aria-expanded="false" menu role="menu" menuitem Edit menuitem Delete menuitem View Details ``` **ARIA Attributes:** - `aria-haspopup="menu"` on trigger; WCAG 4.1.2 - `aria-expanded` on trigger; WCAG 4.1.2 - `aria-controls` if menu id is stable; WCAG 4.1.2 - `role="menu"` on menu; WCAG 4.1.2 - `role="menuitem"` on items; WCAG 4.1.2 - `aria-disabled="true"` only if a disabled menu item remains focusable; WCAG 4.1.2 **Keyboard Interactions:** - Enter/Space: open menu. - Down Arrow: move to next menu item. - Up Arrow: move to previous menu item. - Home/End: optional but preferred. - Escape: close and restore focus. - Enter/Space on item: activate item. **Tests:** - First, middle, and last visible row menus. - Trigger names include row context. - Opening menu sets `aria-expanded="true"`. - Escape restores focus to trigger. - Opening another row menu closes prior menu. - Hidden menus are not reachable by Tab or screen reader navigation. **WCAG Criteria:** - 2.1.1 Keyboard - 2.1.2 No Keyboard Trap - 2.4.3 Focus Order - 2.4.6 Headings and Labels - 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value --- ### Task 6: Screen Reader Manual Test Script 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Files:** - QA screen reader script - NVDA + Chrome checklist **Structure Stub:** ```text NVDA Browse Mode T to table Ctrl+Alt+Arrow cell navigation Tab to controls Enter/Space activation Escape menu close ``` **Tests:** - NVDA announces table caption. - NVDA announces column headers during cell navigation. - NVDA announces sortable headers as buttons. - NVDA announces sort changes. - NVDA announces pagination updates. - NVDA announces row menu expanded/collapsed state. - NVDA menu item navigation is coherent. **WCAG Criteria:** - 1.3.1 Info and Relationships - 2.1.1 Keyboard - 2.4.3 Focus Order - 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value - 4.1.3 Status Messages --- ### Task 7: Responsive and Zoom Tests 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Files:** - Responsive Playwright tests - Manual zoom checklist **Structure Stub:** ```text responsive viewport table scroll container visible focus indicators all controls reachable no full-page horizontal scroll at 200% zoom ``` **Tests:** - 390px viewport table usability. - 768px viewport table usability. - 200% zoom reflow. - Focus ring visibility inside scroll container. - Header cells remain available. - Row menus remain reachable. - Pagination remains reachable. **WCAG Criteria:** - 1.4.10 Reflow - 1.4.11 Non-text Contrast - 2.4.7 Focus Visible - 2.4.11 Focus Not Obscured - 2.4.13 Focus Appearance - 2.5.8 Target Size --- ## a11y-Critic Review Checkpoints | Checkpoint | After Task | Focus Areas | |---|---|---| | 🔍 1 | Task 1 | Confirm axe setup covers default, sorted, paginated, and menu-open states | | 🔍 2 | Task 2 | Verify native table semantics, caption, `scope="col"`, and no overuse of ARIA | | 🔍 3 | Task 3 | Verify `aria-sort` cycle, live announcements, and focus persistence | | 🔍 4 | Task 4 | Verify pagination focus behavior and disabled-state semantics | | 🔍 5 | Task 5 | Verify APG Menu Button behavior, arrow navigation, Escape close, focus restoration | | 🔍 6 | Task 6 | Verify NVDA script tests real table navigation and announcements | | 🔍 7 | Task 7 | Verify responsive behavior, zoom, horizontal scrolling, and focus visibility | --- ## Architecture Overview The test plan covers one complex `DataTable` component with four major accessibility subsystems: - Native semantic table structure - Sortable column buttons with `aria-sort` - Pagination controls with polite status announcements - Per-row APG Menu Button dropdowns Keyboard interaction is split intentionally: - Table data cells are navigated through screen reader table commands, not Tab. - Sort, pagination, and row action controls are reached through Tab. - Row menus use APG menu keyboard behavior after opening. --- ## Acceptance Criteria The DataTable passes this accessibility testing plan when: - Automated axe scans pass in default, sorted, paginated, and row-menu-open states. - Table-specific rules pass: `td-headers-attr`, `th-has-data-cells`, `scope-attr-valid`, `table-duplicate-name`. - The table has a meaningful caption. - All seven headers are ``. - Sortable headers expose correct sort state and cycle none → ascending → descending → none. - Sort changes are announced through one polite live region. - Keyboard focus remains predictable after sorting and pagination. - NVDA + Chrome announces table caption, headers, cells, sort controls, and page updates correctly. - Previous/Next pagination works by keyboard and exposes disabled states correctly. - Row action menus follow APG Menu Button behavior. - Escape closes row menus and restores focus to the trigger. - Responsive layouts preserve all functionality. - At 200% zoom, there is no full-page horizontal scrolling. - Focus indicators remain visible and unobscured. --- ## Failure Modes This plan is designed to prevent: - A table that visually looks correct but lacks usable header associations. - Sort state that changes visually but is not exposed through `aria-sort`. - Live region announcements that are duplicated 50 times because each row has its own live region. - Pagination that reloads rows and drops focus to the document body. - Row menus that open visually but cannot be navigated with arrow keys. - Escape closing a menu without restoring focus. - Hidden menus remaining reachable by keyboard or screen reader. - Responsive horizontal scrolling that traps keyboard users. - QA signoff based only on axe without NVDA table navigation testing. - Over-prescribing `headers` attributes where simple `scope="col"` semantics are sufficient. --- ## References - WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/ - APG Button Pattern: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/button/ - APG Menu Button Pattern: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/menu-button/ - WCAG 2.2 Quick Reference: https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/quickref/ - axe-core Rules: https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/
